note: Pre-build scope and sequencing proposal for Wave 0, 1, and 2. No actual code. No DB schema. No API/endpoint design. This artifact exists to allow Architect to approve the exact build scope and sequencing before build starts.

locked_architectural_rules.rule: BS-ADDR-03 (RunCountryAddressImport) is the ONLY authorized writer for canonical address ingestion (ADDR-E01 ResolvedAddress, ADDR-E02 AddressOwner). This does NOT mean it controls the entire Address aggregate — it only owns the canonical ingestion write path. Other address-related writes (e.g. future address correction flows) require explicit new Architect decision.
BS-ADDR-01 (ResolveAddresses) is read-only against canonical state. It must never write to ADDR-E01 or ADDR-E02 directly. It is only allowed to READ from canonical tables populated by BS-ADDR-03.

Dispatch slices (Wave 6: BS-MSG-04, BS-MSG-06) must not implement hidden fallback logic when ResolveAddresses returns unresolved criteria. Unresolved = explicit UnresolvedCriterion in output. Dispatch creates UnresolvedMessageRecord. Dispatch does NOT silently skip, substitute, or retry — the unresolved outcome is the signal, not an error to suppress.
locked_architectural_rules.source: Wave 2/6 implicit contract — locked in 090 gate (2026-04-14)

waves.name: Customer Foundation
waves.rationale: AGG-CUST Customer/Profile/ApiKey are prerequisite dependencies for Wave 1 (Subscriber → ProfileId), Wave 4 (Pipeline/Prospect → Customer), and Wave 5 (Messaging Core → EvaluateProfileGate). Wave 0 has no upstream dependencies. All three slices are self-contained and can be built simultaneously.

downstream_consumers: Wave 1 — BS-SUB-01 ManageStandardReceiver (ProfileId existence check via MapToProfile)

build_slices.parallel_group: A (all Wave 0 slices are group A)
build_notes: Implement CreateCustomer, UpdateCustomer, CreateProfile, UpdateProfile, DeactivateCustomer, DeactivateProfile as separate operations within one handler boundary
CustomerAccount is a value-object companion to Customer — not a separate entity with independent lifecycle
Soft-delete on Customer/Profile (IsDeleted flag) — never hard delete

build_slices.sequential_constraint: NONE — can build parallel to BS-CUST-01 but should be tested against BS-CUST-01 data
build_notes: Pure projection — zero state mutation. Any state mutation in this handler is a design error.
Returns EffectiveProfilePermissions for all 5 role variants (BR023-BR027) — must be testable with seeded Profile + role assignment data from BS-CUST-01

build_slices.sequential_constraint: NONE — isolated, no dependency on BS-CUST-01 except CustomerId existence
build_notes: OWASP A02: use PBKDF2 or bcrypt for hash. SHA-256+salt is acceptable minimum. Never MD5/SHA1.
CleartextKey returned in CreateKey response only. It must not appear in logs or exception messages.
Revoke = set IsActive=false, not delete — preserves audit trail

parallel_vs_sequential.must_run_sequential: BS-CUST-02 integration tests depend on data from BS-CUST-01 — sequential for test purposes, not for implementation

key_risks.description: 5 role flags (BR023-BR027) each override different fields. Risk: missing a combination in handler logic.
key_risks.mitigation: Unit test matrix: 5 roles × 2 states (present/absent) × each affected field. All 10 combinations verified.

key_risks.description: Developer uses SHA-256 without salt → reversible rainbow table attack (OWASP A02).
key_risks.mitigation: Code review gate: hash implementation must use salt. Unit test: creating two keys with same value produces different hashes.

waves.rollback_isolation: Wave 0 is fully isolated — no effects on downstream until Wave 1+ is built. Rollback = remove Wave 0 migrations and feature code. Zero risk of cascading impact.

validation_checkpoint.description: Wave 0 is complete and Wave 1 may begin when ALL of these pass:
required_tests: BS-CUST-01: Customer CRUD — CreateCustomer, UpdateCustomer, CreateProfile, DeactivateCustomer — all pass
BS-CUST-02: EvaluateProfileGate — returns correct EffectiveProfilePermissions for all 5 role combinations (BR023-BR027)
BS-CUST-02: Profile not found → returns null/not-found result, does NOT throw unhandled exception
BS-CUST-03: CreateKey returns cleartext once; second call to retrieve key fails (not stored)
BS-CUST-03: Two CreateKey calls with same description produce different hashes (salt verified)
BS-CUST-03: RevokeKey is idempotent — revoking already-revoked key returns success
validation_checkpoint.what_must_be_proven_before_wave_1: Customer and Profile entities are creatable, readable, and correctly reflect role assignments for EvaluateProfileGate. ProfileId exists as a stable foreign key reference.

waves.name: Subscriber Foundation
waves.rationale: AGG-SUB StandardReceiver is referenced by BS-MSG-02 (AddRecipientCriterion — existence check) and by BS-IMP-02 as the first concrete import target. Wave 1 depends on Wave 0 for ProfileId validation in the MapToProfile operation. BS-SUB-02 (ManageReceiverPricing) has no cross-slice dependency and can be built in parallel.

upstream_dependencies: Wave 0 complete — BS-CUST-01 (ProfileId must exist for MapToProfile operation in BS-SUB-01)
downstream_consumers: Wave 3 — BS-IMP-02 ConfirmImportJob (BS-SUB-01 is the first concrete import target via COORD-04)

build_slices.sequential_constraint: REQUIRES Wave 0 complete for ProfileId validation
build_notes: Implement as operation-based handler: CreateReceiver, UpdateReceiver, DeactivateReceiver, AddGroup, AddKeyword, AddDistributionPhone, MapToProfile
Keyword uniqueness per country enforced at application layer. DB unique constraint is backup — application must throw KeywordConflictException (not expose DB constraint error to caller).
MapToProfile operation: verify ProfileId exists in AGG-CUST before creating mapping — use in-process call to BS-CUST-01 read path, not a direct FK dependency

build_slices.parallel_group: B (can build in parallel with BS-SUB-01)
build_slices.sequential_constraint: NONE — no dependency on BS-SUB-01 for implementation. Integration tests should use BS-SUB-01-created receivers.
build_notes: UPSERT semantics: INSERT if not exists, UPDATE if composite key (customerId, standardReceiverId, countryId) already present
No business logic beyond the upsert — deliberately simple

parallel_vs_sequential.must_run_sequential: BS-SUB-01 integration tests that test MapToProfile require Wave 0 Profile data. BS-SUB-02 integration tests that test pricing on real receivers require BS-SUB-01 data — sequential for test realism only.

key_risks.description: Two concurrent AddKeyword operations for same keyword/countryId — both pass application-layer check, both attempt insert. Risk: duplicate keyword created.
key_risks.mitigation: DB unique constraint on (keyword, countryId) as final guard. Application layer catches constraint violation and re-throws as KeywordConflictException — not a generic error.

key_risks.description: MapToProfile checks ProfileId existence. Risk: validation bypasses or leaks internal AGG-CUST internals.
key_risks.mitigation: ProfileId lookup via in-process read (not direct table join). StandardReceiver handler must not reference Customer tables directly.

waves.rollback_isolation: Wave 1 is isolated from Wave 0 data — StandardReceiver tables are separate from Customer tables. Rollback = remove Wave 1 migrations and feature code. Wave 0 remains intact.

validation_checkpoint.description: Wave 1 is complete and Wave 3 / Wave 5 may use Subscriber dependency when ALL of these pass:
required_tests: BS-SUB-01: CreateReceiver, AddGroup, AddKeyword, AddDistributionPhone all pass
BS-SUB-01: AddKeyword — duplicate keyword for same countryId returns KeywordConflictException (not DB error)
BS-SUB-01: MapToProfile — ProfileId does not exist → returns not-found error, mapping not created
BS-SUB-01: DeactivateReceiver — receiver is soft-deactivated, not deleted
BS-SUB-02: UPSERT — creating price for new composite key succeeds
BS-SUB-02: UPSERT — updating price for existing composite key succeeds (idempotent)
BS-SUB-02: UPSERT — two concurrent upserts for same key produce exactly one final record (last-writer-wins, no duplicate)
validation_checkpoint.what_must_be_proven_before_wave_3_and_wa: StandardReceiver entities are creatable with keywords, groups, and profile mappings. StandardReceiverId is a stable reference for Messaging AddRecipientCriterion. ReceiverPriceSetting upsert is idempotent.

waves.name: Address Infrastructure
waves.rationale: Address infrastructure has no dependency on Wave 0 or Wave 1 — it can be built fully in parallel with Wave 1. However, within Wave 2 itself the slices must be built in order: BS-ADDR-03 (import + canonical state writer) must be built first, since BS-ADDR-01 and BS-ADDR-02 read from the canonical state that BS-ADDR-03 populates. Wave 2 is a prerequisite for Wave 6 dispatch.

dependency_confirmation.upstream_dependencies: NONE — Wave 2 is independent of Wave 0 and Wave 1

build_slices.parallel_group: C-first (must be built before BS-ADDR-01 and BS-ADDR-02)
build_slices.sequential_constraint: Build first within Wave 2. BS-ADDR-01 and BS-ADDR-02 depend on canonical state data written by this slice.
build_notes: RULE-ADDR-01 applies here: BS-ADDR-03 is the authorized writer for canonical address ingestion (ADDR-E01 ResolvedAddress, ADDR-E02 AddressOwner) — not for the entire Address aggregate
Each country adapter (NO: Kartverket, SE: Lantmäteriet, DK: Datafordeleren) resolves country-native keys to Kvhx INSIDE the adapter. Core never receives country-native keys.
FI (Finland): NullAdapter — throw NotSupportedException. Do NOT add conditional routing delays — fail immediately with clear error.
ADDR-E05 NorwegianAddress, ADDR-E06 SwedishAddress are adapter-internal staging tables. They must not be accessible via any public repository interface.
ADDR-E03 AddressKvhxChange: create a record on every detected Kvhx rename — no exception. This is an append-only audit trail.

build_slices.sequential_constraint: Requires BS-ADDR-03 to exist (and have seeded canonical data for integration tests)
build_notes: RULE-ADDR-02 applies: read-only against canonical ADDR-E01. Must NOT write ResolvedAddress or AddressOwner under any code path.
Internally dispatches to correct IAddressAdapter by countryId — this is an internal implementation detail. No caller knows which adapter was used.
Output contract: resolved = [{criterionId, kvhx, phone?, ownerName?}], unresolved = [{criterionId, reason}]. Both lists are ALWAYS present — caller receives the complete picture.
RULE-DISPATCH-01 applies here (for Wave 6 consumers): unresolved criteria = explicit UnresolvedCriterion in output list. No hidden fallback or retry inside this slice.

build_slices.sequential_constraint: Requires BS-ADDR-03 canonical state to be seeded for integration tests
build_notes: Read-only from ADDR-E02 AddressOwner (canonical) only — NOT from staging tables
Returns ownerType: Private | Business | Unknown — never null
Called only when sendToOwner=true in dispatch context. Can also be called standalone for owner queries.
PN-003: AddressOwner canonical state is populated by BS-ADDR-03. This slice never calls a country adapter directly.

must_run_sequential: BS-ADDR-03 must be complete before BS-ADDR-01 and BS-ADDR-02 integration tests can run meaningfully
parallel_vs_sequential.wave_2_vs_wave_1: Wave 2 and Wave 1 can run fully in parallel — no shared dependencies

key_risks.description: Developer adds a write path to ADDR-E01 from BS-ADDR-01 (e.g. cache-on-first-resolve logic). This violates RULE-ADDR-01 and RULE-ADDR-02.
key_risks.mitigation: Repository interface for ADDR-E01 write must only be injected into BS-ADDR-03 handler. BS-ADDR-01 handler receives read-only IAddressReadRepository. Code review enforces this.

key_risks.description: Country-native key (e.g. Norwegian Koordinatnummer) leaks from adapter into Core layer.
key_risks.mitigation: IAddressAdapter contract returns only [{kvhx, phone?, ownerName?}]. Country-native key must never appear in return type. Unit test: adapter mock returns Kvhx — Core asserts it never sees a country-native key.

key_risks.description: Developer adds silent return-empty logic for FI instead of NotSupportedException — masking the deferred state.
key_risks.mitigation: FI unit test: invoking resolution with countryId=FI must throw NotSupportedException. Empty result is NOT acceptable.

key_risks.description: Developer filters out unresolved criteria from BS-ADDR-01 output to simplify caller code. Wave 6 dispatch then has no visibility into unresolved recipients.
key_risks.mitigation: RULE-DISPATCH-01: output contract always includes unresolved list. Unit test: if one criterion fails resolution, it appears in unresolved list with reason — it does NOT disappear.

waves.rollback_isolation: Wave 2 is isolated — address tables are independent of customer and subscriber tables. Rollback = remove Wave 2 migrations and feature code. Wave 0 and Wave 1 remain intact. Wave 6 (dispatch) cannot function without Wave 2 — Wave 6 must not start until Wave 2 validation checkpoint passes.

validation_checkpoint.description: Wave 2 is complete and Wave 6 may begin when ALL of these pass:
required_tests: BS-ADDR-03: import NO address data → ADDR-E01 canonical state populated with Kvhx records
BS-ADDR-03: import SE address data → canonical state updated
BS-ADDR-03: DK import → canonical state updated
BS-ADDR-03: FI import → NotSupportedException thrown
BS-ADDR-03: re-import of same batch → no duplicate ADDR-E01 records (upsert by Kvhx)
BS-ADDR-03: Kvhx rename detected → ADDR-E03 AddressKvhxChange record created
BS-ADDR-01: known Kvhx criterion → resolved list contains (kvhx, phone)
BS-ADDR-01: unknown Kvhx criterion → unresolved list contains (criterionId, reason). Resolved list does NOT contain it.
BS-ADDR-01: mixed batch (some resolved, some not) → both lists populated correctly
BS-ADDR-01: countryId=FI → NotSupportedException
BS-ADDR-01: does NOT write to ADDR-E01 under any code path (verify via read-only repository interface)
BS-ADDR-02: known Kvhx → returns (ownerName, ownerPhone, ownerType)
BS-ADDR-02: unknown Kvhx → returns Unknown ownerType (not error)
validation_checkpoint.what_must_be_proven_before_wave_6: ResolveAddresses is functional and returns explicit unresolved outcomes. Canonical state is populated and read-only from dispatcher's perspective. RULE-DISPATCH-01 contract is enforced.

parallel_tracks.note: Sequential. Wave 1 depends on Wave 0 for ProfileId.
Fully independent. Can start at same time as Wave 0.

build_order_recommendation.earliest_start_for_waves_3_4_5: Wave 3 can start after Wave 1 complete. Wave 4 can start after Wave 0 complete. Wave 5 can start after Wave 0 + Wave 1 complete.
build_order_recommendation.blocking_dependency_for_wave_6: Wave 6 cannot start until BOTH Wave 5 AND Wave 2 validation checkpoints pass.

what_is_explicitly_out_of_scope_for_wave: No DB schema decisions — schemas are derived from entity design (060) during build phase by the implementing developer
No API endpoint design — endpoints are derived from slice input/output contracts by the implementing developer
